en-US.name: Tattoo Removal: Fade Tracker
en-US.subtitle: Laser Session & Aftercare Log
en-US.promotional_text: Getting laser tattoo removal? Track each session, get today's aftercare step, and see when skin is ready for the next. For your ink, skin, and body.
en-US.description:
Getting laser tattoo removal? Tattoo Removal: Fade Tracker keeps your whole removal journey in one place - how many sessions to expect, what to do after each one, and when your skin is ready for the next session.

Laser tattoo removal runs many sessions over months or years, and two clocks run at once: the aftercare steps that reset after every session, and the recovery time you need before the next one. Both depend on your ink color, ink density, body area, skin tone, and lifestyle - and the answers are rarely obvious. This app does the tracking for you.

HOW IT WORKS
1. Add your tattoo: ink color, density, body area, skin tone.
2. Log each session as you complete it.
3. Get an estimated session range, today's aftercare step, and a GO / WAIT / HOLD read on when to plan your next session.
It takes 30 seconds to set up. No account required.

CORE FEATURES

Session Estimate
See an estimated range of how many sessions your tattoo may take, based on ink color, density, body area, and skin tone.

Today's Aftercare Step
A clear, day-by-day view of the aftercare step for where you are after your latest session.

Next-Session Read
A simple GO / WAIT / HOLD read on whether enough recovery time has passed before your next session, with the days remaining.

PRO FEATURES
Upgrade to Pro for more:
- Track multiple tattoos at once
- Full session history
- Sun-protection window countdown
- Aftercare and window reminders
- Personalized interval that adjusts to your own recovery
- Fade progress photo timeline
- Per-session notes

PRIVACY FIRST
- No account, no login
- All data stored locally on your device
- No tracking, no ads, no third-party analytics

Tattoo Removal: Fade Tracker is an informational scheduling and journaling tool based on general, publicly known removal guidelines. It is not a substitute for the aftercare instructions of your own removal provider or for advice from a licensed clinician.
